title: gen_id sequence counter wraps without detection
description: In id.rs, the AtomicU64 SEQUENCE counter uses fetch_add with Ordering::Relaxed. After 2^64 increments, it wraps to 0, which could produce duplicate IDs if two wraps happen in the same millisecond. This is practically unreachable (would require 18 quintillion operations), and the doc comment says 'good enough for a single-binary server.' But the Relaxed ordering also means the sequence is not monotonic across threads within the same millisecond — two threads could observe the same sequence number if the atomic increment is not yet visible. This is also practically fine for ID uniqueness (the timestamp disambiguates) but the ID comment claims 'lexicographically sortable' which is only true within a single thread. A brief comment acknowledging the cross-thread ordering limitation would prevent future confusion.
close_reason: gen_id doc updated to note Relaxed ordering caveat
